                    <path id="loading-line-bkgd" d="M0,0 L150,0" fill="none" stroke="#F3F2F1" stroke-width="150"></path>
                    <style>
                        #loading-line-main {
                            stroke-dasharray: 150;
                            stroke-dashoffset: 150;
                        }
                        @keyframes animateLine {
                            to {
                                stroke-dashoffset: 0;
                            }
                        }
                        #loading-line-main {
                            animation: animateLine 2s linear infinite;
                        }
                    </style>
                    <path id="loading-line-main" d="M0,0 L150,0" fill="none" stroke="#003A9B" stroke-width="20"></path>

publicado","employerLastReviewed":null,"employerResponsiveCardModel":null,"numOfCandidates":null,"postedToday":false,"recurringHireText":null,"urgentlyHiringModel":null},"hostQueryExecutionResult":{"data":{"jobData":{"__typename":"JobDataPayload","results":[{"__typename":"JobDataResult","trackingKey":"5-cmh1-3-1jo8nqpc922s2l00","job":{"__typename":"Job","key":"f72bbee9a4126416","title":"HPC Software Engineer","sourceEmployerName":"Synopsys","datePublished":1778216400000,"expired":false,"language":"en","normalizedTitle":"software engineer","refNum":"12885","expirationDate":null,"sponsoredEmployerKey":null,"tracking":{"__typename":"JobTrackingResult","jobClick":{"__typename":"JobTrackingJobClickResult","url":"http:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Frc\u002Fclk?jk=f72bbee9a4126416&bb=zPEPK-eVzc9urJnqVl_GqpvdU0qdsCVpuHDTVUj1ZCoSIYP98jyYHUtLuY10rfXpw1J4mqtbbk0SjjyvJNdWHmPNlbT27G7S&xkcb=SoAB67M3jInk9lAXFr0LbzkdCdPP"}},"source":{"__typename":"JobSource","key":"AAAAAR7Z34CmK7xJEWrHDGI4NfgYai+90kFQo3DhnEbVPPs5Rt3IqA==","name":"Synopsys"},"feed":{"__typename":"JobFeed","key":"AAAAAebmXRk1mdlGKA3R8bG25NTWjETxHTlclTjNYMBnQt5L2aTbMw==","isDradis":false,"feedSourceType":"EMPLOYER"},"url":"https:\u002F\u002Fcareers.synopsys.com\u002Fjob\u002F-\u002F-\u002F44408\u002F87085299616","dateOnIndeed":1778329963655,"description":{"__typename":"JobDescription","html":"\u003Cdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nSynopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nYou Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nYou have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nWorking across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nCollaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nAt Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nWhat You'll Be Doing\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nDesign, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003C\u002Fdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nProfile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nBuild andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nDrive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nCollaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nSupport procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nOwn packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nThe Impact You Will Have\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nReduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nEnable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nAccelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nImprove developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nEnsure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nHelp shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nSupport faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment infrastructure\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nWhat You'll Need\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nMinimumRequirements:Bachelor's degree inMechanical Engineering,Computational Science,Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related field with 2+ years of experience, orMaster'sdegree in a related field.PhD preferred.\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nStrong hands-on experience with HPC software design, testing, and deployment in production or research environments\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nSolid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and performance considerations in parallel computing contexts\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nProficiencywith Git and collaborative development workflows across distributed teams\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nProficiencyin Fortran and C\u002FC++ for performance-critical code development\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003C\u002Fdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nExperience with MPI and distributed memory programming models\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nExperience with GPU hardware and at least one GPU programming model such as CUDA, HIP, SYCL\u002FoneAPI, OpenMP,OpenACC, orKokkosis a strong plus\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nWho You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nYou can look at a profiler trace andidentifythe bottleneck without needing three meetings to discuss it\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nYou write code that other engineers can pick up six months later without needing you to explain every design choice\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nYou ask clarifying questions when a requirement is vague rather than guessing and building the wrong thing\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nYou are comfortable managing your own time across multiple priorities and know when to escalate blockers versus solve them yourself\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nYou can explain a technical tradeoff between two HPC approaches to a domain expert in terms they care about, not just what the benchmark says\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nYou stay current with HPC architecture trends and programming models without needing to be told, because you care about building software that will still perform well two hardware generations from now\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nThe Team You'll Be Part Of\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nYou will work within the Ansys Mechanical R&amp;D organization, collaborating with numerical methods experts, solver developers, and other HPC engineers across a geographically distributed team. Your work will directly support the performance and scalability of industry-leading simulation products used by engineers worldwide to design and validate critical systems.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nRewards and Benefits\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nWe offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003C\u002Fdiv>\u003C\u002Fdiv>","text":"We Are\n\nSynopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\n\nYou Are\n\nYou have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\n\nWorking across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\n\nCollaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\n\nAt Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\n\nWhat You'll Be Doing\n\nDesign, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\nProfile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\nBuild andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\nDrive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\nCollaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\nSupport procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\nOwn packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\n\nThe Impact You Will Have\n\nReduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\nEnable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\nAccelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\nImprove developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\nEnsure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\nHelp shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\nSupport faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment infrastructure\n\nWhat You'll Need\n\nMinimumRequirements:Bachelor's degree inMechanical Engineering,Computational Science,Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related field with 2+ years of experience, orMaster'sdegree in a related field.PhD preferred.\nStrong hands-on experience with HPC software design, testing, and deployment in production or research environments\nSolid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and performance considerations in parallel computing contexts\nProficiencywith Git and collaborative development workflows across distributed teams\nProficiencyin Fortran and C\u002FC++ for performance-critical code development\nExperience with MPI and distributed memory programming models\nExperience with GPU hardware and at least one GPU programming model such as CUDA, HIP, SYCL\u002FoneAPI, OpenMP,OpenACC, orKokkosis a strong plus\n\nWho You Are\n\nYou can look at a profiler trace andidentifythe bottleneck without needing three meetings to discuss it\nYou write code that other engineers can pick up six months later without needing you to explain every design choice\nYou ask clarifying questions when a requirement is vague rather than guessing and building the wrong thing\nYou are comfortable managing your own time across multiple priorities and know when to escalate blockers versus solve them yourself\nYou can explain a technical tradeoff between two HPC approaches to a domain expert in terms they care about, not just what the benchmark says\nYou stay current with HPC architecture trends and programming models without needing to be told, because you care about building software that will still perform well two hardware generations from now\n\nThe Team You'll Be Part Of\n\nYou will work within the Ansys Mechanical R&D organization, collaborating with numerical methods experts, solver developers, and other HPC engineers across a geographically distributed team. Your work will directly support the performance and scalability of industry-leading simulation products used by engineers worldwide to design and validate critical systems.\n\nRewards and Benefits\n\nWe offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.","semanticSegments":[]},"location":{"__typename":"JobLocation","countryCode":"US","admin1Code":"PA","admin1Name":"Pennsylvania","admin2Code":"125","admin2Name":"Washington County","admin3Code":null,"admin3Name":null,"admin4Code":null,"admin4Name":null,"city":"Canonsburg","postalCode":null,"latitude":40.26257,"longitude":-80.18728,"streetAddress":null,"fullAddress":"Canonsburg, PA","formatted":{"__typename":"FormattedJobLocation","long":"Canonsburg, PA","short":"Canonsburg, PA"}},"occupations":[{"__typename":"JobOccupation","key":"5NN53","label":"Software Development Occupations"},{"__typename":"JobOccupation","key":"EHPW9","label":"Technology Occupations"},{"__typename":"JobOccupation","key":"HJSX6","label":"Software Development & Architecture Occupations"}],"occupationMostLikelySuids":[{"__typename":"JobOccupation","key":"5NN53"}],"benefits":[],"socialInsurance":[],"workingSystem":[],"requiredDocumentsCategorizedAttributes":[],"indeedApply":{"__typename":"JobIndeedApplyAttributes","key":null,"scopes":[]},"attributes":[{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"2AWWS","label":"GPU programming"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"6BEHV","label":"GPU architecture"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"6QB52","label":"Engineering testing"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"77DPA","label":"Applied Mathematics"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"7DZKQ","label":"Software design"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"9MWQS","label":"Software coding"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"AFFE9","label":"Engineering"},{"__typename":"JobAttribute","key":"AJY84","label":"Data Development Occupations","suid":"5NN53"}}],"attributeIdsMapping":[]}}]}},"errors":[],"query":"query getViewJobJobData($jobKeyString: String!, $shouldQueryCogsEligibility: Boolean!, $shouldQuerySavedJob: Boolean!, $shouldQueryTitleNorm: Boolean!, $shouldQueryCompanyFields: Boolean!, $shouldQuerySalary: Boolean!, $shouldQueryIndeedApplyLink: Boolean!, $shouldQueryParentEmployer: Boolean!, $shouldQuerySentiment: Boolean!, $shouldIncludeFragmentZone: Boolean!, $shouldQueryMatchComparison: Boolean!, $shouldQueryApplicantInsights: Boolean!, $shouldQueryContactEmail: Boolean!, $shouldQueryEmployerOutreachEngagements: Boolean! = false, $shouldQueryApplyRateLimit: Boolean! = false, $shouldQueryJapanInternshipMetadata: Boolean! = false, $source: String!, $mobvjtk: String!, $continueUrl: String!, $spn: Boolean!, $jobDataInput: JobDataInput!, $resumeEngagementsAfter: String = null, $resumeEngagementsInput: FindResumeEngagementsInput! = {}, $clientMeta: String) {\n  jobData(input: $jobDataInput) {\n    __typename\n    results {\n      __typename\n      trackingKey\n      job {\n        __typename\n        key\n        ...jobDataFields\n        ...titleNormFields@include(if: $shouldQueryTitleNorm)\n        ...savedJobFields@include(if: $shouldQuerySavedJob)\n        ...companyFields@include(if: $shouldQueryCompanyFields)\n        ...parentEmployerFields@include(if: $shouldQueryParentEmployer)\n        jobSearchMobileMigrationData @include(if: $shouldQuerySentiment) {\n          __typename\n          knownUserSentimentIdMap {\n            __typename\n            id\n            suid\n          }\n          userStringDislikedPreferences {\n            __typename\n            uid\n          }\n          userMinimumPayPreferences {\n            __typename\n            currency\n            payAmount\n            salaryType\n          }\n          preferencesFetchStatus\n          paySentiment\n        }\n        jobTypes: attributes(customClass: \"BM62A\") {\n          __typename\n          key\n          label\n          sentiment @include(if: $shouldQuerySentiment)\n        }\n        shiftAndSchedule: attributes(customClass: \"BTSWR\") {\n          __typename\n          key\n          label\n          sentiment @include(if: $shouldQuerySentiment)\n        }\n        compensation @include(if: $shouldQuerySalary) {\n          __typename\n          key\n        }\n        indeedApply @include(if: $shouldQueryIndeedApplyLink) {\n          __typename\n          applyLink(property: INDEED, trackingKeys: {mobvjtk : $mobvjtk}, continueUrl: $continueUrl, spn: $spn, clientMeta: $clientMeta, source: $source) {\n            __typename\n            url\n            iaUid\n          }\n        }\n        ...japanInternshipFields@include(if: $shouldQueryJapanInternshipMetadata)\n        ...ViewJobFragmentZone@include(if: $shouldIncludeFragmentZone)\n        applicants @include(if: $shouldQueryApplicantInsights) {\n          __typename\n          hasInsights\n          ...ApplicantDemographicsFields\n        }\n      }\n      matchComparisonResult @include(if: $shouldQueryMatchComparison) {\n        __typename\n        attributeComparisons {\n          __typename\n          jobProvenance\n          attribute {\n            __typename\n            interestedParty\n            label\n            lastModified\n            suid\n            profileAttributeTypeSuid\n          }\n          jobRequirementStrength\n          jsProvenance\n          matchType\n        }\n        encouragementToApply {\n          __typename\n          score\n          strategy\n          trafficLight\n        }\n        occupationComparisons {\n          __typename\n          jsProvenance\n          matchType\n          occupation {\n            __typename\n            label\n            suid\n          }\n        }\n        attributeIdsMapping {\n          __typename\n          id\n          suid\n          sentiment\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  cogsCheckEligibility(input: {jobKey : $jobKeyString}) @include(if: $shouldQueryCogsEligibility) {\n    __typename\n    error\n    isEligible\n  }\n  jobSeekerProfile @include(if: $shouldQueryApplicantInsights) {\n    __typename\n    profile {\n      __typename\n      subscription {\n        __typename\n        status\n      }\n      jsProPremiumAccess {\n        __typename\n        applicantInsights {\n          __typename\n          access\n          branding\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  findResumeEngagements(first: 50, after: $resumeEngagementsAfter, input: $resumeEngagementsInput) @include(if: $shouldQueryEmployerOutreachEngagements) {\n    __typename\n    engagements {\n      __typename\n      id\n      sender {\n        __typename\n        name\n        email\n        id\n      }\n      status\n      createdDate\n      type\n      recipient {\n        __typename\n        id\n        email\n        phoneNumber\n      }\n      job {\n        __typename\n        title\n        company\n        description\n        employerJobId\n        htmlDescription\n      }\n      conversation {\n        __typename\n        key\n        initialMessage {\n          __typename\n          id\n          textBody\n          htmlBody\n        }\n      }\n      statusHistory {\n        __typename\n        status\n        timestamp\n        modifiedDate\n      }\n      feedback {\n        __typename\n        declineReason\n        comment\n      }\n      fullApplyRequested\n    }\n  }\n  assessRiskForContexts(input: {contexts : [JS_APPLY_START_RATE_LIMIT]}) @include(if: $shouldQueryApplyRateLimit) {\n    __typename\n    action\n    limitInfo {\n      __typename\n      limit\n      remaining\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfragment jobDataFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  title\n  sourceEmployerName\n  datePublished\n  expired\n  language\n  normalizedTitle\n  refNum\n  expirationDate\n  sponsoredEmployerKey\n  tracking {\n    __typename\n    jobClick {\n      __typename\n      url\n    }\n  }\n  source {\n    __typename\n    key\n    name\n  }\n  feed {\n    __typename\n    key\n    isDradis\n    feedSourceType\n  }\n  url\n  dateOnIndeed\n  description {\n    __typename\n    html\n    text\n    semanticSegments {\n      __typename\n      content\n      header\n      label\n    }\n  }\n  location {\n    __typename\n    countryCode\n    admin1Code\n    admin1Name\n    admin2Code\n    admin2Name\n    admin3Code\n    admin3Name\n    admin4Code\n    admin4Name\n    city\n    postalCode\n    latitude\n    longitude\n    streetAddress\n    fullAddress\n    formatted {\n      __typename\n      long\n      short\n    }\n  }\n  occupations {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  occupationMostLikelySuids: occupations(occupationOption: MOST_LIKELY) {\n    __typename\n    key\n  }\n  benefits: attributes(customClass: \"SXMYH\") {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  socialInsurance: attributes(customClass: \"KTQQ7\") {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  workingSystem: attributes(customClass: \"TY4DY\") {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  requiredDocumentsCategorizedAttributes: attributes(customClass: \"PQCTE\") {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  indeedApply {\n    __typename\n    key\n    scopes\n  }\n  attributes {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  employerProvidedAttributes: attributes(sources: [EMPLOYER]) {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n  employerProvidedOccupations: occupations(sources: [EMPLOYER], occupationOption: MOST_SPECIFIC) {\n    __typename\n    label\n  }\n  hiringDemand {\n    __typename\n    isUrgentHire\n    isHighVolumeHiring\n  }\n  thirdPartyTrackingUrls\n  jobSearchMobileMigrationData {\n    __typename\n    advertiserId\n    jobTypeId\n    applyEmail\n    applyUrl\n    contactEmail @include(if: $shouldQueryContactEmail)\n    contactPhone\n    isNoUniqueUrl\n    urlRewriteRule\n    mapDisplayType\n    jobTags\n    locCountry\n    waldoVisibilityLevel\n    isKnownSponsoredOnDesktop\n    isKnownSponsoredOnMobile\n    tpiaAdvNum\n    tpiaApiToken\n    tpiaCoverLetter\n    tpiaEmail\n    tpiaFinishAppUrl\n    tpiaJobCompanyName\n    tpiaJobId\n    tpiaJobLocation\n    tpiaJobMeta\n    tpiaJobTitle\n    tpiaLocale\n    tpiaName\n    tpiaPhone\n    tpiaPostUrl\n    tpiaPresent\n    tpiaResume\n    tpiaResumeFieldsOptional\n    tpiaResumeFieldsRequired\n    tpiaScreenerQuestions\n    iaVisibility\n    hasPreciseLocation\n    algoKey\n  }\n  jobStats {\n    __typename\n    organicApplyStarts\n  }\n  isRepost\n  isLatestPost\n  isPlacement\n  photos {\n    __typename\n    w400\n    w800\n    w1600\n    altText\n  }\n}\n\nfragment titleNormFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  normalizedTitle\n  displayTitle\n}\n\nfragment savedJobFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  savedJob {\n    __typename\n    state\n    currentStateTime\n    applicationDraft {\n      __typename\n      expiryTime\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfragment companyFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  employer {\n    __typename\n    key\n    tier\n    relativeCompanyPageUrl\n    dossier {\n      __typename\n      images {\n        __typename\n        rectangularLogoUrl\n        headerImageUrls {\n          __typename\n          url1960x400\n        }\n        squareLogoUrls {\n          __typename\n          url256\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    ugcStats {\n      __typename\n      globalReviewCount\n      ratings {\n        __typename\n        overallRating {\n          __typename\n          count\n          value\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfragment parentEmployerFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  employer {\n    __typename\n    subsidiaryOptOutStatusV2\n    parentEmployer {\n      __typename\n      name\n      subsidiaryOptOutStatusV2\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfragment japanInternshipFields on Job {\n  __typename\n  internship {\n    __typename\n    periodRange {\n      __typename\n      periodMin\n      periodMax\n    }\n    startMonth {\n      __typename\n      year\n      month\n    }\n  }\n  applicationDueDate\n  graduationYear\n  programType: attributes(keys: [\"XZD3C\", \"BWM3E\"], sources: [EMPLOYER]) {\n    __typename\n    key\n    label\n  }\n}\n\nfragment ViewJobFragmentZone on Job {\n  key\n  __typename\n}\n\nfragment ApplicantDemographicsFields on Applicants {\n  __typename\n  count {\n    __typename\n    total\n    last24h\n  }\n  workExperience {\n    __typename\n    title\n    pct\n  }\n  education {\n    __typename\n    title\n    pct\n  }\n  skills {\n    __typename\n    title\n    pct\n  }\n  certificates {\n    __typename\n    title\n    pct\n  }\n  previewInsight {\n InfoModel":null,"jobDescriptionSectionModel":null,"jobInfoHeaderModel":{"companyImagesModel":{"ejiBannerAsBackground":false,"enhancedJobDescription":false,"featuredEmployer":false,"headerImageUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fd2q79iu7y748jz.cloudfront.net\u002Fs\u002F_headerimage\u002F1960x400\u002F7e8823cb2dd2527ad3d249b9516ce7fb","logoAltText":"Synopsys logo","logoImageOverlayLower":false,"logoUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fd2q79iu7y748jz.cloudfront.net\u002Fs\u002F_squarelogo\u002F256x256\u002F7531f895f1e40aa308767f32255b6a69","showBannerTop":false,"showEnhancedJobImp":false,"showIconInTitle":false,"showJpBrandLogo":false},"companyName":"Synopsys","companyOverviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys?campaignid=mobvjcmp&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416","companyReviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","companyReviewModel":{"companyName":"Synopsys","desktopCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=viewjob&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=viewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","mobileCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","navigableContainerModel":{"contentHtml":null,"hasBorderBottom":true,"hasBorderTop":true,"href":null,"isRTL":false},"ratingsModel":{"ariaContent":"4 de 5 estrellas. Enlace a las Evaluaciones de empresa de 337 (se abre en una nueva pestaña)","count":337,"countContent":"337 evaluaciones","descriptionContent":"Lee opiniones sobre trabajar en esta empresa.","rating":4,"showCount":true,"showDescription":true,"size":null}},"employerActivity":null,"employerProvidedOccupations":[],"employerResponsiveCardModel":null,"encryptedFccCompanyId":null,"formattedLocation":"Canonsburg, PA","hideRating":false,"isCondensedRating":true,"isDesktopApplyButtonSticky":false,"isSimpleVjImproveActive":true,"isSimplifiedHeader":false,"jobFlairLabelModel":null,"jobNormTitle":null,"jobTitle":"HPC Software Engineer","jobTypes":null,"location":null,"openCompanyLinksInNewTab":false,"parentCompanyName":null,"ratingsModel":null,"recentSearch":null,"remoteLocation":false,"remoteWorkModel":null,"salaryCurrency":null,"salaryMax":null,"salaryMin":null,"salaryType":null,"subtitle":"Synopsys - Canonsburg, PA","tagModels":null,"taxonomyAttributes":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"jobMetadataHeaderModel":{"jobType":""},"jobTagModel":null,"resumeEvaluationResult":null,"sanitizedJobDescription":"\u003Cdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Synopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Working across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Collaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>At Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Be Doing\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Design, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Profile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Build andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Drive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Collaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Own packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Impact You Will Have\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Reduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Enable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Accelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Improve developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Ensure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Help shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment infrastructure\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Need\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>MinimumRequirements:Bachelor's degree inMechanical Engineering,Computational Science,Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related field with 2+ years of experience, orMaster'sdegree in a related field.PhD preferred.\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Strong hands-on experience with HPC software design, testing, and deployment in production or research environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and performance considerations in parallel computing contexts\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencywith Git and collaborative development workflows across distributed teams\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencyin Fortran and C\u002FC++ for performance-critical code development\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with MPI and distributed memory programming models\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with GPU hardware and at least one GPU programming model such as CUDA, HIP, SYCL\u002FoneAPI, OpenMP,OpenACC, orKokkosis a strong plus\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Who You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>You can look at a profiler trace andidentifythe bottleneck without needing three meetings to discuss it\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You write code that other engineers can pick up six months later without needing you to explain every design choice\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You ask clarifying questions when a requirement is vague rather than guessing and building the wrong thing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You are comfortable managing your own time across multiple priorities and know when to escalate blockers versus solve them yourself\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You can explain a technical tradeoff between two HPC approaches to a domain expert in terms they care about, not just what the benchmark says\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You stay current with HPC architecture trends and programming models without needing to be told, because you care about building software that will still perform well two hardware generations from now\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Team You'll Be Part Of\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You will work within the Ansys Mechanical R&amp;D organization, collaborating with numerical methods experts, solver developers, and other HPC engineers across a geographically distributed team. Your work will directly support the performance and scalability of industry-leading simulation products used by engineers worldwide to design and validate critical systems.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Rewards and Benefits\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>We offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.\u003C\u002Fp>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n\u003C\u002Fdiv>","showExpiredHeader":false,"structuredDataJobSummaryModel":null,"tagModels":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"sectionedJobInfoModel":null},"jobKey":"f72bbee9a4126416","jobLanguage":"en","jobLocation":"Canonsburg, PA","jobMetadataFooterModel":{"advertiserName":"","age":"Recién .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs{box-sizing:border-box;background:none;appearance:none;text-align:start;text-decoration:none;border:none;cursor:pointer;user-select:none;align-items:center;justify-content:center;position:relative;margin-block-start:0;margin-block-end:0;margin-inline-start:0;margin-inline-end:0;padding-inline-start:1rem;padding-inline-end:1rem;line-height:1.5;font-family:'Indeed 1);display:inline-flex;inline-size:auto;padding-block-start:0.5625rem;padding-block-end:0.5625rem;min-block-size:2.75rem;color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs::-moz-focus-inner{border:0;}@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs[aria-disabled='true']{cursor:default;}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:visited{color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:hover:not([aria-disabled='true']){color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak-hover);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:active:not([aria-disabled='true']){color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);box-shadow:var(--ifl-shadows-inset);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak-active);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs{box-sizing:border-box;background:none;appearance:none;text-align:start;text-decoration:none;border:none;cursor:pointer;user-select:none;align-items:center;justify-content:center;position:relative;margin-block-start:0;margin-block-end:0;margin-inline-start:0;margin-inline-end:0;padding-inline-start:1rem;padding-inline-end:1rem;line-height:1.5;font-family:'Indeed 0.045, 0.355, 1),background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.645, 0.045, 0.355, 1),opacity 200ms cubic-bezier(0.645, 0.045, 0.355, 1),box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.645, 0.045, 0.355, 1),color 200ms 1);display:inline-flex;inline-size:auto;padding-block-start:0.5625rem;padding-block-end:0.5625rem;min-block-size:2.75rem;color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs::-moz-focus-inner{border:0;}@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs[aria-disabled='true']{cursor:default;}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:visited{color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:hover:not([aria-disabled='true']){color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak-hover);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent .mosaic-provider-reportcontent-1hfwffs:active:not([aria-disabled='true']){color:var(--ifl-colors-foreground-strong);box-shadow:var(--ifl-shadows-inset);background-color:var(--ifl-colors-background-action-weak-active);}.mosaic-provider-reportcontent logo","logoImageOverlayLower":false,"logoUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fd2q79iu7y748jz.cloudfront.net\u002Fs\u002F_squarelogo\u002F256x256\u002F7531f895f1e40aa308767f32255b6a69","showBannerTop":false,"showEnhancedJobImp":false,"showIconInTitle":false,"showJpBrandLogo":false},"companyName":"Synopsys","companyOverviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys?campaignid=mobvjcmp&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416","companyReviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","companyReviewModel":{"companyName":"Synopsys","desktopCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=viewjob&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=viewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","mobileCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","navigableContainerModel":{"contentHtml":null,"hasBorderBottom":true,"hasBorderTop":true,"href":null,"isRTL":false},"ratingsModel":{"ariaContent":"4 Enlace a las Evaluaciones de empresa de 337 (se abre en una nueva pestaña)","count":337,"countContent":"337 evaluaciones","descriptionContent":"Lee opiniones sobre trabajar en esta empresa.","rating":4,"showCount":true,"showDescription":true,"size":null}},"employerActivity":null,"employerProvidedOccupations":[],"employerResponsiveCardModel":null,"encryptedFccCompanyId":null,"formattedLocation":"Canonsburg, PA","hideRating":false,"isCondensedRating":true,"isDesktopApplyButtonSticky":false,"isSimpleVjImproveActive":true,"isSimplifiedHeader":false,"jobFlairLabelModel":null,"jobNormTitle":null,"jobTitle":"HPC Software Engineer","jobTypes":null,"location":null,"openCompanyLinksInNewTab":false,"parentCompanyName":null,"ratingsModel":null,"recentSearch":null,"remoteLocation":false,"remoteWorkModel":null,"salaryCurrency":null,"salaryMax":null,"salaryMin":null,"salaryType":null,"subtitle":"Synopsys - Canonsburg, PA","tagModels":null,"taxonomyAttributes":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"jobMetadataHeaderModel":{"jobType":""},"jobTagModel":null,"resumeEvaluationResult":null,"sanitizedJobDescription":"\u003Cdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Synopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Working across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Collaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>At Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Be Doing\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Design, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Profile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Build andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Drive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Collaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Own packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Impact You Will Have\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Reduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Enable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Accelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Improve developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Ensure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Help shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment infrastructure\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Need\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>MinimumRequirements:Bachelor's degree inMechanical Engineering,Computational Science,Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related field with 2+ years of experience, orMaster'sdegree in a related field.PhD preferred.\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Strong hands-on experience with HPC software design, testing, and deployment in production or research environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and performance considerations in parallel computing contexts\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencywith Git and collaborative development workflows across distributed teams\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencyin Fortran and C\u002FC++ for performance-critical code development\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with MPI and distributed memory programming models\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with GPU hardware and at least one GPU programming model such as CUDA, HIP, SYCL\u002FoneAPI, OpenMP,OpenACC, orKokkosis a strong plus\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Who You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>You can look at a profiler trace andidentifythe bottleneck without needing three meetings to discuss it\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You write code that other engineers can pick up six months later without needing you to explain every design choice\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You ask clarifying questions when a requirement is vague rather than guessing and building the wrong thing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You are comfortable managing your own time across multiple priorities and know when to escalate blockers versus solve them yourself\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You can explain a technical tradeoff between two HPC approaches to a domain expert in terms they care about, not just what the benchmark says\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You stay current with HPC architecture trends and programming models without needing to be told, because you care about building software that will still perform well two hardware generations from now\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Team You'll Be Part Of\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You will work within the Ansys Mechanical R&amp;D organization, collaborating with numerical methods experts, solver developers, and other HPC engineers across a geographically distributed team. Your work will directly support the performance and scalability of industry-leading simulation products used by engineers worldwide to design and validate critical systems.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Rewards and Benefits\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>We offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.\u003C\u002Fp>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n\u003C\u002Fdiv>","showExpiredHeader":false,"structuredDataJobSummaryModel":null,"tagModels":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"sectionedJobInfoModel":null},"mobtk":"1jo8nqp2hggol800","profileBaseUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fprofile.indeed.com","rtl":false,"salaryInfoModel":null},"resumeWordCount":0,"jobDescription":"\u003Cdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nSynopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nYou Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nYou have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nWorking across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nCollaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nAt Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nCollaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software logo","logoImageOverlayLower":false,"logoUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fd2q79iu7y748jz.cloudfront.net\u002Fs\u002F_squarelogo\u002F256x256\u002F7531f895f1e40aa308767f32255b6a69","showBannerTop":false,"showEnhancedJobImp":false,"showIconInTitle":false,"showJpBrandLogo":false},"companyName":"Synopsys","companyOverviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys?campaignid=mobvjcmp&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416","companyReviewLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","companyReviewModel":{"companyName":"Synopsys","desktopCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=viewjob&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=viewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","mobileCompanyLink":"https:\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Fcmp\u002FSynopsys\u002Freviews?campaignid=mobvjcmp&cmpratingc=mobviewjob&from=mobviewjob&tk=1jo8nqpasggol800&fromjk=f72bbee9a4126416&jt=HPC+Software+Engineer","navigableContainerModel":{"contentHtml":null,"hasBorderBottom":true,"hasBorderTop":true,"href":null,"isRTL":false},"ratingsModel":{"ariaContent":"4 Enlace a las Evaluaciones de empresa de 337 (se abre en una nueva pestaña)","count":337,"countContent":"337 evaluaciones","descriptionContent":"Lee opiniones sobre trabajar en esta empresa.","rating":4,"showCount":true,"showDescription":true,"size":null}},"employerActivity":null,"employerProvidedOccupations":[],"employerResponsiveCardModel":null,"encryptedFccCompanyId":null,"formattedLocation":"Canonsburg, PA","hideRating":false,"isCondensedRating":true,"isDesktopApplyButtonSticky":false,"isSimpleVjImproveActive":true,"isSimplifiedHeader":false,"jobFlairLabelModel":null,"jobNormTitle":null,"jobTitle":"HPC Software Engineer","jobTypes":null,"location":null,"openCompanyLinksInNewTab":false,"parentCompanyName":null,"ratingsModel":null,"recentSearch":null,"remoteLocation":false,"remoteWorkModel":null,"salaryCurrency":null,"salaryMax":null,"salaryMin":null,"salaryType":null,"subtitle":"Synopsys - Canonsburg, PA","tagModels":null,"taxonomyAttributes":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"jobMetadataHeaderModel":{"jobType":""},"jobTagModel":null,"resumeEvaluationResult":null,"sanitizedJobDescription":"\u003Cdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Synopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Working across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>Collaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>At Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Be Doing\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Design, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Profile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Build andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Drive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Collaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Own packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Impact You Will Have\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Reduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Enable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Accelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Improve developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Ensure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Help shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Support faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment infrastructure\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> What You'll Need\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>MinimumRequirements:Bachelor's degree inMechanical Engineering,Computational Science,Applied Mathematics, Physics, or related field with 2+ years of experience, orMaster'sdegree in a related field.PhD preferred.\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Strong hands-on experience with HPC software design, testing, and deployment in production or research environments\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and performance considerations in parallel computing contexts\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencywith Git and collaborative development workflows across distributed teams\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Proficiencyin Fortran and C\u002FC++ for performance-critical code development\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n \u003Cdiv>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with MPI and distributed memory programming models\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>Experience with GPU hardware and at least one GPU programming model such as CUDA, HIP, SYCL\u002FoneAPI, OpenMP,OpenACC, orKokkosis a strong plus\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Who You Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cul>\n   \u003Cli>You can look at a profiler trace andidentifythe bottleneck without needing three meetings to discuss it\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You write code that other engineers can pick up six months later without needing you to explain every design choice\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You ask clarifying questions when a requirement is vague rather than guessing and building the wrong thing\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You are comfortable managing your own time across multiple priorities and know when to escalate blockers versus solve them yourself\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You can explain a technical tradeoff between two HPC approaches to a domain expert in terms they care about, not just what the benchmark says\u003C\u002Fli>\n   \u003Cli>You stay current with HPC architecture trends and programming models without needing to be told, because you care about building software that will still perform well two hardware generations from now\u003C\u002Fli>\n  \u003C\u002Ful>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> The Team You'll Be Part Of\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>You will work within the Ansys Mechanical R&amp;D organization, collaborating with numerical methods experts, solver developers, and other HPC engineers across a geographically distributed team. Your work will directly support the performance and scalability of industry-leading simulation products used by engineers worldwide to design and validate critical systems.\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>\u003Cb> Rewards and Benefits\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\n  \u003Cp>We offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.\u003C\u002Fp>\n \u003C\u002Fdiv>\n\u003C\u002Fdiv>","showExpiredHeader":false,"structuredDataJobSummaryModel":null,"tagModels":null,"viewJobDisplay":"DESKTOP_EMBEDDED"},"sectionedJobInfoModel":null},"mobtk":"1jo8nqp2hggol800","profileBaseUrl":"https:\u002F\u002Fprofile.indeed.com","rtl":false,"salaryInfoModel":null},"jobDescription":"\u003Cdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>We Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nSynopsys is the leader in engineering solutions from silicon to systems, enabling customers to rapidly innovate AI-powered products. We deliver industry-leading silicon design, IP, simulation and analysis solutions, and design services. We partner closely with our customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&amp;D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nYou Are\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nYou have spent your career making code run faster on hardware that keeps changing. You understand that the difference between a solver that takes three hours and one that takes twenty minutes is not just compute power, it is how the algorithm maps to memory, how threads communicate, and whether someone thought hard about cache locality six months ago. You know your way around a profiler and you can read the output without needing to Google every metric.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nWorking across CPU and GPU architectures does not intimidate you. You have debugged MPI communication bottlenecks, wrestled with build systems that span multiple platforms, and written code that has to perform consistently whether it is running on a local workstation or a cloud-based HPC cluster. You think in terms of scalability, not just correctness.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nCollaboration comes naturally. You can sit with a domain expert who speaks in finite elements and boundary conditions, extract what matters for the code, and turn that into a design spec that your team can actually build from. You care about the engineers downstream who depend on your work holding up under real-world load, not just passing CI.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\nAt Synopsys, you will work on simulation software that shapes how products get designed across industries. The problems are real, the scale is significant, and what you optimize will matter.\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nWhat You'll Be Doing\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nDesign, implement, andoptimizeparallel programming methods within Ansys Mechanical solver products using MPI, GPU programming models like CUDA, HIP, SYCL, OpenMP, and other HPC frameworks\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003C\u002Fdiv>\u003Cdiv>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nProfile solver performance across CPU and GPU architectures using tools like IntelVTune, NVIDIA Nsight, or similar, and translate findings into actionable performance improvements\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nBuild andmaintaincode benchmarking suites that track solver performance across releases and catch regressions before they ship\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nDrive adoption of modular, hardware-agnostic HPC programming models across multiple solver codebases, working with development teams to ensure consistency and reusability\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nCollaborate with numerical methods experts to translate complex algorithmic requirements into performant, maintainable software designs\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nSupport procurement, configuration, and management of HPC development and testing platforms, includingon-premiseclusters and cloud-based environments\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nOwn packaging, build system work, and DevOps tooling usingCMake, Azure DevOps, Conan, Docker, or CI\u002FCD pipelines to streamline deployment and testing workflows\u003C\u002Fli>\u003C\u002Ful>\u003Cp>\u003Cb>\nThe Impact You Will Have\u003C\u002Fb>\u003C\u002Fp>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>\nReduce solve times for engineering simulations used by leading companies across automotive, aerospace, energy, and electronics industries\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nEnable customers to run larger, more complex models by making solvers scale efficiently across hundreds or thousands of cores\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nAccelerate the adoption of GPU computing in production simulation workflows, unlocking new performance tiers for users with modern hardware\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nImprove developer productivity across multiple solver teams by building reusable HPC frameworks and shared tooling\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nEnsure performance consistency and reliability across solver releases through rigorous benchmarking and regression testing\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nHelp shape the technical direction of Synopsys simulation products as HPC architectures and customer workloads continue to evolve\u003C\u002Fli>\u003Cli>\nSupport faster iteration cycles for product development teams by streamlining build, test, and deployment 17 12.7508 17H11.2508C11.1127 17 11.0008 16.8881 11.0008 16.75V15.25Z","isLastAndMaxPage":false,"resultsInfoModalCloseAriaLabel":"cerrar icono","resultsInfoModalInnerContent":"\u003Cdiv class='jobsearch-ResultsInfo-text'>Aquí se muestran los anuncios de empleo que coinciden con tu búsqueda. Es posible que estas empresas remuneren a Indeed, lo que permite que Indeed siga siendo gratis para los candidatos. Indeed ordena los anuncios de empleo en función de las remuneraciones que las empresas le pagan y de su relevancia, según los términos de búsqueda que empleaste y tu actividad en Indeed. Para obtener más información, consulta las \u003Ca href=\"\u002F\u002Fwww.indeed.com\u002Flegal?hl=es#tosIntro\">Condiciones del servicio de Indeed\u003C\u002Fa>.\u003C\u002Fdiv>","resultsInfoModalTitle":"¿Cómo se clasifican estos empleos?","serpVisualHeaderText":"Empleos de software developer en United States","showWhenScrollUp":false,"totalNumResults":466,"totalNumResultsFormatted":"466"},"searchUID":"1jo8nqp2vggol800","serpBottomResumePromoModel":null,"serpGroupingsTstValue":-1,"serpHeadingVariant":null,"serpUpdatePaginationButtonTstValue":-1,"shouldLoadRNVJWeb":false,"shouldUseZustand":false,"showOmittedJobsUrl":null,"stagingLevel":"prod","staticPrefix":"https:\u002F\u002Fc03.s3.indeed.com\u002Fhpfe\u002Findeedmobile-statics\u002Fs\u002F","timestamp":1778409890912,"totalJobCount":466,"uniqueJobsCount":466,"usePreStateKeyUUIDV4Seed":true,"deploymentGroup":"jsf"};

            // marker property that this was rendered by HPFE/JSF rather than Mobile
            window._jsf = true;

            window.addEventListener('pageshow', function(e) {
                if (window.performance && typeof window.performance.getEntriesByType === 'function') {
                    const perfEntries = window.performance.getEntriesByType('navigation');
                    if (perfEntries && perfEntries.length > 0 && perfEntries[0].type === 'back_forward') {
                        window['_bk'] = '1';
                    }
                }
                if (e.persisted) {
                    window['_bk'] = '1';
                }
            });
        </script>

style="height:800px"><style>
                            @keyframes fadeOutAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    opacity: 1;
                                }
                                to {
                                    opacity: 0;
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes fadeInAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    opacity: 0;
                                }
                                to {
                                    opacity: 1;
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes moveUpAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    transform: translateY(0);
                                }
                                to {
                                    transform: translateY(-0px);
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes moveDownAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    transform: translateY(-0px);
                                }
                                to {
                                    transform: translateY(0);
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes embeddedViewJobMoveUpAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    margin-top: 0px;
                                }
                                to {
                                    margin-top: -15px
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes jobTitleMoveUpAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    transform: scale(1) translateY(0px);
                                    transform-origin: 0 0;
                                }
                                to {
                                    transform: scale(0.9) translateY(-0px);
                                    transform-origin: 0 0;
                                }
                            }

                            @keyframes jobTitleMoveDownAnimationKeyframes {
                                from {
                                    transform: scale(0.9) translateY(-0px);
                                    transform-origin: 0 0;
                                }
                                to {
                                    transform: scale(1) translateY(0px);
                                    transform-origin: 0 0;
                                }
                            }
